what: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
text: Adjust monetary values inflation across 13 currencies 60+ years. Convert historical prices today’s money, calculate inflation rates, reverse calculation find modern amount worth past. Uses CPI GDP deflator indices World Bank. Getting consistent, comparable inflation data across multiple countries many decades harder sounds. country national statistics agency, methodology, publication format. World Bank Development Indicators solve aggregating data national sources single, consistently formatted dataset - World Bank handling source reconciliation. series rescaled 2020 = 100 consistency across currencies.

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
text: inflateR runtime dependencies requires API key internet connection. data bundled directly inside package.
code: install.packages("inflateR")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/inflateR")

what: CPI vs GDP deflator - which should I use?
title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
text: inflateR provides two methods adjusting monetary values across time. Choosing depends ’re adjusting. Use CPI (adjust_inflation, historical_value) : - Wages salaries - “£30,000 salary 1990 worth today?” - Everyday prices - “£12 1963 worth today’s money?” - Household budgets cost living comparisons - Retail prices, rents, consumer spending CPI tracks price fixed basket goods services typical household buys. ’s intuitive measure anything person earns spends. Use GDP deflator (adjust_real, historical_real) : - GDP figures - “UK GDP 1980 compare today real terms?” - Government expenditure - “2000 defence budget worth today?” - Business investment capital expenditure - macroeconomic aggregate want compare across time GDP deflator covers goods services produced economy - just consumer basket. updates automatically (fixed basket) excludes imported goods, making appropriate economy-wide comparisons. doubt: ’re talking something person earns, buys, pays - use CPI. ’re talking number national accounts table - use GDP deflator.

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
text: inflateR provides six functions: four adjustment functions two symmetric pairs, plus inflation_rate() list_currencies(): four adjustment functions accept currency codes country names exact inverses within pair.
code: library(inflateR)  # CPI: adjust a historical value to today's money adjust_inflation(amount, from_year, currency, to_year = NULL, round = 2)  # CPI: convert a present value back to a historical year historical_value(amount, to_year, currency, from_year = NULL, round = 2)  # GDP deflator: adjust a historical value to today's money adjust_real(amount, from_year, currency, to_year = NULL, round = 2)  # GDP deflator: convert a present value back to a historical year historical_real(amount, to_year, currency, from_year = NULL, round = 2)  # Calculate cumulative or annualised inflation rate inflation_rate(from_year, to_year = NULL, currency, annualise = FALSE)  # See all supported currencies and data coverage list_currencies()

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
code: # What is £12 from 1963 worth today? adjust_inflation(12, 1963, "GBP") #> [1] 256.43  # What is $50 USD from 1980 worth today? adjust_inflation(50, 1980, "USD") #> [1] 190.33  # What is AUD 100 from 1990 worth today? adjust_inflation(100, 1990, "AUD") #> [1] 241.39  # Adjust to a specific year rather than today adjust_inflation(100, 1970, "GBP", to_year = 2000) #> [1] 872.45  # What would £100 today have been worth in 1963? historical_value(100, 1963, "GBP") #> [1] 4.68  # What would USD 1000 in 2020 have been worth in 1990? historical_value(1000, 1990, "USD", from_year = 2020) #> [1] 504.80

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
code: # UK GDP was roughly £500bn in 1990 - what is that in today's terms? adjust_real(500e9, 1990, "GBP") #> [1] 1214415929203  # What would $1 trillion of US government spending in 2000 be worth in 2020? adjust_real(1e12, 2000, "USD", to_year = 2020) #> [1] 1428890000000  # Reverse: what would today's UK GDP of £2.5 trillion have been in 1990 terms? historical_real(2.5e12, 1990, "GBP") #> [1] 1028928849648

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
code: # Cumulative US inflation from 2000 to 2020 inflation_rate(2000, 2020, "USD") #> [1] 0.5030  # Annualised UK inflation from 1990 to 2020 inflation_rate(1990, 2020, "GBP", annualise = TRUE) #> [1] 0.0248

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
code: # Adjust multiple amounts at once adjust_inflation(c(100, 500, 1000), 2000, "GBP", to_year = 2020) #> [1]  161.56  807.80 1615.61  # Full precision (no rounding) adjust_inflation(100, 2000, "USD", to_year = 2020, round = NULL) #> [1] 150.303...

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
code: # See all supported currencies list_currencies() #>    currency                      country cpi_start cpi_end deflator_start deflator_end #> 1       GBP               United Kingdom      1960    2024           1960         2024 #> 2       AUD                    Australia      1960    2024           1960         2024 #> ...

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
text: data sourced World Bank Development Indicators bundled inside package. indices rescaled 2020 = 100.

title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
text: Tax shocks - Australia (GST, 2000) Canada (GST, 1991) saw one-time price level jumps appear CPI data really structural tax changes. Comparisons spanning years reflect tax shift, just underlying inflation. Japan’s deflation - Japan near-zero negative inflation roughly 1995 2020. Adjustments within window small, years prices actually fell. China’s CPI coverage - World Bank CPI data China begins 1986. GDP deflator series begins 1960 early years span significant structural change. series internationally comparable may capture full experience price changes China’s reform era. Euro proxy - World Bank publish aggregated Euro area series either CPI GDP deflator. Germany used proxy , reflects monetary anchor Eurozone understate inflation experience southern European countries 1970s 1980s. Annual figures - values annual averages. Month--month volatility smoothed .

what: Why do my results differ slightly from other calculators?
title: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values
text: Inflation adjustment requires converting 12 monthly CPI observations single yearly figure. CPI January vs December year can differ 1-2%, conversion done matters tool use. Annual vs monthly CPI. inflateR (online calculators like Minneapolis Fed) uses annual average CPI: mean 12 monthly values year. BLS’s calculator lets pick specific months, can shift results 1-2% depending choice. biggest source differences calculators. Index level vs percentage change. inflateR uses World Bank’s CPI index level (FP.CPI.TOTL), published rounded 2 decimal places. tools may use annual percentage change series (FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG), retains decimal places compounded year year. come underlying national CPI data. rounding difference tiny: $100,000 2020 2024 gives $121,200 inflateR vs $121,204 compounding approach, gap $4 (0.003%). ## Related packages inflateR package part suite R packages economic, financial, policy data. share consistent interface (named functions, tidy data frames, local caching) designed work together. Data access: Analytical toolkits:

what: Adjust a historical monetary value for inflation — adjust_inflation
title: Adjust a historical monetary value for inflation — adjust_inflation
text: Converts amount historical year equivalent value target year, using bundled CPI data sourced World Bank Development Indicators. Supports GBP, AUD, USD, EUR, CAD, JPY, CNY, CHF, NZD, INR, KRW, BRL, NOK.

title: Adjust a historical monetary value for inflation — adjust_inflation
code: adjust_inflation(amount, from_year, currency, to_year = NULL, round = 2)

title: Adjust a historical monetary value for inflation — adjust_inflation
text: amount Numeric (scalar vector). original monetary amount(s). from_year Integer. year amount . currency Character. currency code country name. Accepted codes: "GBP", "AUD", "USD", "EUR", "CAD", "JPY", "CNY", "CHF", "NZD", "INR", "KRW", "BRL", "NOK". Country names also accepted, e.g. "Australia", "United States", "Japan", "New Zealand", "India", "Norway" (case-insensitive). to_year Integer. target year adjust . Defaults latest available year data. round Integer NULL. Number decimal places round (default 2). Use NULL full precision.

title: Adjust a historical monetary value for inflation — adjust_inflation
text: numeric value (vector) representing inflation-adjusted amount.

title: Adjust a historical monetary value for inflation — adjust_inflation
code: # What is £12 from 1963 worth today? adjust_inflation(12, 1963, "GBP") #> [1] 256.43  # What is AUD 50 from 1980 worth in 2000? adjust_inflation(50, 1980, "AUD", to_year = 2000) #> [1] 135.59  # Full precision adjust_inflation(100, 2000, "USD", to_year = 2020, round = NULL) #> [1] 150.3081

what: Adjust a monetary value using a GDP deflator — adjust_real
title: Adjust a monetary value using a GDP deflator — adjust_real
text: Converts amount two years using GDP deflator data sourced World Bank Development Indicators (indicator: NY.GDP.DEFL.ZS). Suitable adjusting GDP figures, government expenditure, macroeconomic aggregates. adjusting personal consumer values (wages, prices goods), use adjust_inflation uses CPI instead.

title: Adjust a monetary value using a GDP deflator — adjust_real
code: adjust_real(amount, from_year, currency, to_year = NULL, round = 2)

title: Adjust a monetary value using a GDP deflator — adjust_real
text: amount Numeric (scalar vector). original monetary amount(s). from_year Integer. year amount . currency Character. currency code country name. Accepted codes: "GBP", "AUD", "USD", "EUR", "CAD", "JPY", "CNY", "CHF", "NZD", "INR", "KRW", "BRL", "NOK". Country names also accepted, e.g. "Australia", "United States", "Japan", "Switzerland" (case-insensitive). to_year Integer. target year adjust . Defaults latest available year deflator series. round Integer NULL. Number decimal places round (default 2). Use NULL full precision.

title: Adjust a monetary value using a GDP deflator — adjust_real
text: numeric value (vector) representing deflator-adjusted amount.

title: Adjust a monetary value using a GDP deflator — adjust_real
text: GDP deflator measures price changes across goods services produced economy, unlike CPI tracks fixed consumer basket. Key differences CPI: Covers domestic production, just consumer goods Excludes imported goods (CPI includes ) Updates basket automatically (CPI uses fixed basket) Published annually/quarterly (CPI monthly) Use GDP deflator comparing macroeconomic aggregates (GDP, government spending, investment) across time. Use adjust_inflation personal consumer values.

title: Adjust a monetary value using a GDP deflator — adjust_real
code: # Adjust UK GDP from 1990 to today using GDP deflator adjust_real(500000, 1990, "GBP") #> [1] 1214416  # Compare US government spending in 2000 vs 2020 terms adjust_real(1000000, 2000, "USD", to_year = 2020) #> [1] 1428890

what: Convert a value to its historical equivalent using a GDP deflator — historical_real
title: Convert a value to its historical equivalent using a GDP deflator — historical_real
text: Takes monetary amount recent year returns worth specified historical year, using GDP deflator data sourced World Bank Development Indicators (indicator: NY.GDP.DEFL.ZS). inverse adjust_real.

title: Convert a value to its historical equivalent using a GDP deflator — historical_real
code: historical_real(amount, to_year, currency, from_year = NULL, round = 2)

title: Convert a value to its historical equivalent using a GDP deflator — historical_real
text: amount Numeric (scalar vector). monetary amount(s) reference year. to_year Integer. historical year convert back . currency Character. Currency code ("GBP", "AUD", "USD", "EUR", "CAD", "JPY", "CNY", "CHF", "NZD", "INR", "KRW", "BRL", "NOK") country name ("Australia", "United States", etc.) — case-insensitive. from_year Integer. year amount . Defaults latest year available deflator series. round Integer NULL. Number decimal places round (default 2). Use NULL full precision.

title: Convert a value to its historical equivalent using a GDP deflator — historical_real
text: numeric value (vector) representing historical equivalent amount.

title: Convert a value to its historical equivalent using a GDP deflator — historical_real
text: converting consumer personal values, use historical_value uses CPI instead.

title: Convert a value to its historical equivalent using a GDP deflator — historical_real
code: # What would UK GDP of £2 trillion today have been in 1990 terms? historical_real(2e12, 1990, "GBP") #> [1] 823440738647  # What would USD 1 trillion in 2020 have been worth in 2000? historical_real(1e12, 2000, "USD", from_year = 2020) #> [1] 6.99844e+11

what: inflateR: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values — inflateR-package
title: inflateR: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values — inflateR-package
text: Convert historical monetary values present-day equivalents using bundled CPI (Consumer Price Index) GDP deflator data sourced World Bank Development Indicators. Supports British pounds (GBP), Australian dollars (AUD), US dollars (USD), Euro (EUR), Canadian dollars (CAD), Japanese yen (JPY), Chinese yuan (CNY), Swiss francs (CHF), New Zealand dollars (NZD), Indian rupees (INR), South Korean won (KRW), Brazilian reais (BRL), Norwegian krone (NOK). Currency codes country names accepted input.

title: inflateR: Inflation Adjustment for Historical Currency Values — inflateR-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

what: Calculate the inflation rate between two years — inflation_rate
title: Calculate the inflation rate between two years — inflation_rate
text: Returns cumulative annualised inflation rate two years, using bundled CPI data sourced World Bank Development Indicators.

title: Calculate the inflation rate between two years — inflation_rate
code: inflation_rate(from_year, to_year = NULL, currency, annualise = FALSE)

title: Calculate the inflation rate between two years — inflation_rate
text: from_year Integer. starting year. to_year Integer. ending year. Defaults latest available year data. currency Character. currency code country name (case-insensitive). See adjust_inflation accepted values. annualise Logical. TRUE, returns annualised (compound annual) rate. FALSE (default), returns cumulative rate.

title: Calculate the inflation rate between two years — inflation_rate
text: numeric value representing inflation rate proportion (e.g. 0.35 means 35\

title: Calculate the inflation rate between two years — inflation_rate
code: # Cumulative US inflation from 2000 to 2020 inflation_rate(2000, 2020, "USD") #> [1] 0.5030813  # Annualised UK inflation from 1990 to 2020 inflation_rate(1990, 2020, "GBP", annualise = TRUE) #> [1] 0.02298728

title: List supported currencies and data coverage — list_currencies

title: inflateR 0.2.0
text: Added inflation_rate() calculating cumulative annualised inflation rates two years Added list_currencies() show supported currencies data coverage Added round parameter four adjustment functions (round = 2 default preserves backward compatibility; use round = NULL full precision) adjustment functions now accept vector amount inputs Refactored internal code: extracted shared helpers eliminate 4x code duplication across functions Added comprehensive test suite (80+ tests covering functions, 13 currencies, error handling, inverse properties, vectorisation) Updated data-raw/cpi_data.R cover 13 currencies CPI GDP deflator series single script

title: inflateR 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-04 Added 5 new currencies: NZD (New Zealand), INR (India), KRW (South Korea), BRL (Brazil), NOK (Norway) Bundled CPI GDP deflator data new currencies (World Bank series) BRL CPI coverage begins 1980; new series cover 1960–2024

title: inflateR 0.1.2
text: Added adjust_real() GDP deflator-based adjustment (World Bank NY.GDP.DEFL.ZS) Bundled GDP deflator data (1960–2024) 8 supported currencies adjust_real() accepts currency codes country names adjust_inflation()

title: inflateR 0.1.0
text: Initial release Supports inflation adjustment GBP, AUD, USD, EUR, CAD, JPY, CNY, CHF Bundled CPI data 1960–2024 currencies (CNY 1986) Currency codes country names accepted input (case-insensitive) Data sourced World Bank Development Indicators (FP.CPI.TOTL), rescaled 2020 = 100
